---
description: "Full pre-commit workflow for xspct_db: format, lint, REUSE check, tests, stage changes, and suggest a properly tagged GPG-signed commit message."
agent: agent
tools: [execute, read, search]
---

Run the complete pre-commit workflow for xspct_db and suggest a commit message.

## Steps (run in order — stop and report on failure)

### 1. Format
```bash
.venv/bin/ruff format src/ tests/
.venv/bin/ruff check --select I --fix src/ tests/
```
Report any files reformatted.

### 2. Lint
```bash
.venv/bin/ruff check src/ tests/
```
If there are lint errors, **stop** and report them. Do not proceed until the user resolves them.

### 3. REUSE / SPDX compliance
```bash
.venv/bin/reuse lint
```
Every source file must have lines 1–2:
```
# SPDX-License-Identifier: EUPL-1.2
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: <year> Carsten Rosenberg <c.rosenberg@heinlein-support.de>
```
If reuse is not installed, scan manually for missing headers. Stop on violations.

### 4. Tests
```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -v --tb=short
```
If any tests fail, **stop** and show the full failure output. Do not proceed.

### 5. Stage formatted / fixed files
```bash
git add -u
```
List the files staged.

### 6. Suggest a commit message

Ask the user (or infer from the changes) what was done, then suggest a commit message using the
**`[Tag] Description`** format:

| Tag | When to use |
|-----|-------------|
| `[Feature]` | New user-visible feature |
| `[Fix]` | Bug fix |
| `[Minor]` | Small/trivial change (whitespace, nil check, typo) |
| `[Rework]` | Major refactoring |
| `[Conf]` | Configuration change |
| `[Test]` | Test-only change |
| `[Docs]` | Documentation only |
| `[Project]` | Build system, CI, packaging |

Example: `[Feature] Add foreground/background query queue with 503/504 responses`

### 7. GPG signing reminder

**ALL commits must be GPG-signed:**
```bash
git commit -S -m "[Tag] Description"
```
Remind the user to use `-S`. Tags must also be signed: `git tag -s X.Y.Z -m "message"`.

## Hard rules

- **NEVER** include "generated by", "co-authored by", or any AI attribution in the commit message.
- Keep the commit message subject line under 72 characters.
- Use the imperative mood ("Add …", "Fix …", not "Added …" or "Fixes …").
